prerequisites.prereq: 11th pass experiment completed (or Phase 1 results available)

issue-review.title: GPU capacity constraints
issue-review.assessment: europe_strict with 1 L4 GPU quota in europe-west4 creates a hard bottleneck. Jobs cycle PENDING/STARTING indefinitely when the single L4 is unavailable. With 22 jobs to run sequentially (1 GPU quota), even if GPUs are available, wall-clock time is ~22 x avg_duration. When GPUs are NOT available, the experiment stalls entirely with no automatic fallback.
issue-review.root-cause: Three factors compound: (1) europe_strict locks to a single region with limited L4 pool, (2) GCP quota of 1 L4 GPU means zero parallelism, (3) SkyPilot has no mechanism to notify when capacity becomes available.
issue-review.proposal: 1. Create a new region config "europe_relaxed" that includes europe-west1 and europe-west3 as fallbacks (still EU-only, minimal egress ~$0.01/GiB). 2. Make europe_relaxed the default for debug runs. europe_strict remains available for cost-sensitive production runs. 3. Request GCP quota increase for NVIDIA_L4_GPUS from 1 to 4 in europe-west4. This allows 4-way parallelism and reduces wall-clock by 4x. 4. Add a preflight check that reads the region config and warns if only 1 region is configured AND GPU quota is 1 (bottleneck detection).

issue-review.title: SkyPilot controller auto-stop
issue-review.assessment: The controller auto-stops after 10 min idle (AUTOSTOP=10m). Before each experiment, the user must restart it, adding 2-3 min latency. If the user forgets, preflight fails. The controller costs ~EUR 93-100/month when running 24/7, so auto-stop is a cost control — but 10 min is too aggressive.
issue-review.root-cause: SkyPilot default auto-stop is 10 minutes. The project never changed this because the controller was only recently moved to GCP (post-migration).
issue-review.proposal: 1. Increase auto-stop to 60 minutes during active experiment sessions. This covers the typical gap between experiment preparation and launch. 2. Add a --ensure-controller flag to run_factorial.sh that calls "sky jobs controller start" if the controller is stopped. This makes controller state transparent to the user. 3. For overnight/weekend idle, keep auto-stop at 60 min — the controller will stop naturally after the last job completes, saving ~EUR 3/day. 4. Document the "start controller" step in cold-start instructions as a preflight gate, not a manual step.

issue-review.title: No per-model spot/on-demand override
issue-review.assessment: RESOLVED in this session. run_factorial.sh now reads model_overrides.use_spot from the factorial YAML and passes --no-use-spot to sky jobs launch for SAM3 models. Verified via dry-run. No further action needed — document the mechanism for future reference.

issue-review.title: SAM3 weights downloaded from HuggingFace every job
issue-review.assessment:
SAM3 zero-shot jobs are getting spot-preempted during setup because the setup phase takes ~15-20 min: Docker pull from GAR (~5 min, 6 GB), SAM3 weights from HuggingFace (~10 min, 9 GB external network), DVC data from GCS (~3 min, same-region). The HuggingFace download is the longest single step and uses external network (slow, variable latency, no GCP peering). Every preempted job restarts the full setup — compounding the problem.
Solution: cache pretrained weights on GCS in europe-west4. Same-region transfer is free and fast (1-2 min for 9 GB vs 10 min from HuggingFace). This reduces total setup from ~15-20 min to ~8-10 min, making spot preemption recovery much faster and reducing the window of vulnerability.
issue-review.root-cause: train_factorial.yaml setup currently calls hf_hub_download('facebook/sam3', 'sam3.pt') on every job launch. This downloads 9 GB over the public internet each time. No caching layer exists. Combined with the 6 GB Docker pull, the setup phase exceeds the typical spot preemption grace period.
issue-review.proposal: 1. One-time upload: gsutil cp sam3.pt gs://minivess-mlops-checkpoints/pretrained/sam3.pt 2. Update SkyPilot YAML setup to use: gsutil cp gs://minivess-mlops-checkpoints/pretrained/sam3.pt /app/pretrained/ instead of hf_hub_download. Same-region transfer: free, ~1-2 min. 3. Keep HuggingFace as fallback: if GCS copy fails, fall back to hf_hub_download. 4. This is NOT an artifact (trained output) — it is a pretrained INPUT weight. Does NOT violate the mlflow_only_artifact_contract. 5. Storage cost: ~$0.05/month for 9 GB on GCS Standard.

issue-review.title: Docker image freshness check false positives
issue-review.assessment: The preflight check_docker_image_freshness() compares the image's org.opencontainers.image.revision label against git HEAD. When HEAD moves for doc/test-only changes (no src/ changes), the image is functionally identical but the check fails. This forces unnecessary Docker rebuilds (~20 min each) and blocks experiment launches.
issue-review.root-cause: The freshness check uses git HEAD as the reference, but HEAD includes ALL commits (docs, tests, configs, metalearning). The Docker image only contains src/, deployment/, configs/ — so doc-only commits create a false mismatch.
issue-review.proposal: 1. Replace HEAD comparison with a "content hash" of Docker-relevant paths. Compute: git diff --stat HEAD..IMAGE_COMMIT -- src/ deployment/docker/ configs/ pyproject.toml uv.lock. If the diff is empty, the image is fresh. 2. Implementation in scripts/preflight_gcp.py:check_docker_image_freshness(): - Get IMAGE_COMMIT from the OCI label (existing code). - Run "git diff --name-only IMAGE_COMMIT..HEAD -- src/ deployment/docker/ configs/ pyproject.toml uv.lock" to check if Docker-relevant files changed. - If no Docker-relevant files changed, return PASS with message "Image commit {X} differs from HEAD {Y} but no Docker-relevant files changed." - If Docker-relevant files DID change, return FAIL as today. 3. Add a Docker-relevant paths list as a constant in preflight_gcp.py: DOCKER_RELEVANT_PATHS = ["src/", "deployment/docker/", "configs/", "pyproject.toml", "uv.lock"]. 4. Add test: test_docker_freshness_ignores_doc_changes.

issue-review.title: Stale .venv paths after repo move
issue-review.assessment: Moving the repo from github-personal/ to knowledge-hub/repositories/ broke .venv shebangs. Every script that uses #!/path/to/.venv/bin/python had the old absolute path baked in. This caused SkyPilot API server to fail on startup.
issue-review.root-cause: Python venvs store absolute paths in shebangs and pyvenv.cfg. Moving the parent directory invalidates all of them. The fix is always "recreate the venv" but there is no automated detection.
issue-review.proposal: 1. Add a preflight check that validates .venv/pyvenv.cfg "home" path matches the current repo location. If mismatched, print "Stale venv detected. Run: rm -rf .venv && uv sync --all-extras". 2. Implementation in scripts/preflight_gcp.py: new check_venv_paths() function. Read .venv/pyvenv.cfg, parse "home = /path/to/python", verify the path exists. 3. Add this check to the cold-start instructions as CS0 (before all other checks).

issue-review.title: SkyPilot API version mismatch
issue-review.assessment: The SkyPilot API server was running v1.0.0.dev20260314 (12 days behind) while the client was v1.0.0.dev20260326. This happened after venv recreation — the client was updated but the server process was stale.
issue-review.root-cause: "sky api start" launches a background process from the venv. After "uv sync" updates the SkyPilot package, the running server process still uses the old version. There is no automatic server restart on package update.
issue-review.proposal: 1. Add a preflight check that compares "sky api version" (server) with "sky --version" (client). If mismatched, print "SkyPilot API server version mismatch. Run: sky api stop && sky api start". 2. Better: have the venv freshness check (issue G) also trigger "sky api stop && sky api start" when the venv is recreated. 3. Add to cold-start instructions: after uv sync, always restart SkyPilot API.

issue-review.title: DVC data not on GCS after region migration
issue-review.assessment: After migrating from europe-north1 to europe-west4, all 3 GCS buckets were empty. The Pulumi stack created new buckets in the new region but DVC data was never re-pushed. This caused a 45-min delay: download MiniVess from EBRAINS (5 min), push to GCS (2 min), discover DeepVess also missing (10 min), download from Cornell eCommons (4 min), push to GCS (2 min), plus debugging.
issue-review.root-cause: The region migration plan (gcp-spot-with-skypilot-and-pulumi-up-plan.xml) focused on infrastructure (GAR, Cloud SQL, Cloud Run) but did not include a "re-push data to new buckets" step. The DVC remote URL changed (same bucket names, new region) but the data content was not migrated. Pulumi creates empty buckets — it does not copy data.
issue-review.proposal: 1. Create a post-migration checklist script: scripts/post_migration_verify.py. Checks: (a) DVC data on new GCS remote, (b) Docker images on new GAR, (c) MLflow experiments accessible, (d) Cloud SQL has expected databases. 2. Enhance preflight_gcp.py: the existing check_dvc_data_on_gcs() already verifies data presence. Ensure it runs BEFORE any launch and has a clear remediation message: "DVC data not on GCS. Run: dvc push -r gcs". 3. Add a "data migration" section to the Pulumi post-up hook. After "pulumi up" succeeds, print: "Infrastructure created. Now push data: dvc push -r gcs && gsutil cp -r data/external/ gs://minivess-mlops-dvc-data/external/". 4. For DeepVess specifically: the download_deepvess() function now exists in src/minivess/data/downloaders.py. Register it in the acquisition flow so "prefect deployment run 'acquisition-flow/default' --params '{dataset: deepvess}'" handles the full download + push pipeline.

issue-review.title: No automated DeepVess downloader
issue-review.assessment:
RESOLVED in this session. download_deepvess() implemented in src/minivess/data/downloaders.py, registered in _DOWNLOADERS dict, acquisition_registry.py updated from download_method="manual" to "http_download". 3 tests written and passing.
However, the acquisition flow integration is incomplete: the downloader function exists but the Prefect acquisition flow does not yet call it automatically for DeepVess. The function must be manually invoked.
issue-review.root-cause: The acquisition flow's downloader dispatch was designed for MiniVess only. When DeepVess was added as a test dataset, the download_method was set to "manual" as a placeholder. Nobody implemented the automated path.
issue-review.proposal: 1. Wire download_deepvess() into the acquisition flow's downloader dispatch. The dispatch in src/minivess/flows/acquisition_flow.py should check _DOWNLOADERS[dataset_name] and call it automatically. 2. Add VesselNN downloader as well (drift detection dataset). All 3 known datasets (MiniVess, DeepVess, VesselNN) should have automated downloaders. 3. Add an "acquisition flow smoke test" that verifies all registered datasets have a working downloader (mock HTTP calls, verify dispatch). 4. Add a DVC push step after download: download → format_convert → dvc add → dvc push.

issue-review.title: SAM3 pretrained weights not cached on GCS
issue-review.assessment: Pretrained model weights are INPUTS to the pipeline, analogous to training data. Currently SAM3 weights (9 GB) are fetched from HuggingFace on every job launch — an external network dependency that adds ~10 min to setup. The gs://minivess-mlops-checkpoints bucket already exists in europe-west4 and can store pretrained weights under a /pretrained/ prefix. Same-region GCS transfer is free and ~5x faster than cross-network HuggingFace download.
issue-review.proposal: 1. Create gs://minivess-mlops-checkpoints/pretrained/ prefix for all pretrained model weights (SAM3 today, VesselFM in future). 2. One-time upload: download sam3.pt locally, gsutil cp to the bucket. 3. Update SkyPilot setup block: gsutil cp gs://...pretrained/sam3.pt /app/ 4. Add pretrained weight presence to post_migration_verify.py checks. 5. Storage cost: ~$0.05/month (9 GB Standard, europe-west4).

issue-review.title: GPU capacity constraints (reliability perspective)
issue-review.assessment: The PENDING/STARTING cycle is a liveness issue. Jobs appear to be "running" from SkyPilot's perspective but are actually cycling through failed provisioning attempts. There is no alerting — the user must manually poll "sky jobs queue" to discover the stall. The max_wait_hours=168 (1 week) means a stuck experiment could burn controller costs for a week before auto-cancelling.
issue-review.proposal: 1. Add a "provisioning stall detector" to the monitoring protocol. If a job has been in PENDING/STARTING for >30 min with 0 successful provisions, emit a WARNING with the message: "Job X has been cycling PENDING/STARTING for {N} min. Possible causes: (a) GPU capacity exhausted in {region}, (b) quota insufficient, (c) image pull failing." 2. Reduce max_wait_hours from 168 (1 week) to 24 (1 day) for debug runs. A debug experiment should not silently wait a week. Production runs can keep 168h. Add max_wait_hours to debug.yaml infrastructure section. 3. Add a preflight GPU availability check: query SkyPilot for current spot availability in the configured regions. "sky gpus --cloud gcp L4" shows real-time availability. If 0 available in all configured regions, warn the user BEFORE launching. 4. Implement a simple notification mechanism: when Phase 1 jobs have been PENDING for >15 min, write a status file to outputs/experiment_status.txt that can be polled by external tools or checked manually.

issue-review.title: SkyPilot controller auto-stop (reliability perspective)
issue-review.assessment: The auto-stop is a reliability risk during long experiments. If the controller stops mid-experiment (unlikely with active jobs, but possible during inter-phase gaps), in-flight job monitoring is lost. The controller manages job state — if it dies, job status cannot be queried until restart.
issue-review.proposal: 1. The controller should NOT auto-stop while any jobs are in PENDING, STARTING, RUNNING, or RECOVERING state. SkyPilot likely already handles this, but verify by checking SkyPilot documentation. 2. Add a "controller health" check to the polling protocol: every 5 min during experiment execution, verify "sky jobs queue" returns successfully. If it fails, the controller may have stopped — restart automatically. 3. Add controller status to the experiment report: "Controller: UP (europe-west1-b, n4-standard-4, uptime: 2h 15m)".

issue-review.title: SAM3 weight download during setup (reliability perspective)
issue-review.assessment: The 15-20 min setup window is the primary reliability risk for SAM3 spot jobs. Spot preemption can occur at any time — the longer the setup, the higher the probability of preemption before useful work begins. Each preemption triggers a FULL re-download (Docker pull + weights + data), creating a positive feedback loop: long setup → preemption → restart → long setup again. Caching weights on GCS cuts setup by ~7-8 min, significantly reducing the preemption vulnerability window.
issue-review.proposal: 1. Cache on GCS: reduces external network dependency from 2 (Docker + HuggingFace) to 1 (Docker from GAR only). Both Docker and weights become same-region. 2. Add setup-time tracking: log the timestamp at setup start and at each substep (docker pull, weight download, data pull) to measure improvement. 3. Consider pre-pulling Docker images to a GCE disk snapshot for further setup reduction (future optimization, not blocking).

issue-review.title: SkyPilot API version mismatch (reliability perspective)
issue-review.assessment: API version mismatches can cause subtle bugs: the client sends requests the server does not understand, or the server returns responses the client cannot parse. This is especially dangerous with dev builds (v1.0.0.dev*) where the API is not yet stable.
issue-review.proposal: 1. Make version mismatch a HARD FAIL in preflight, not just a warning. If server != client, refuse to launch and print the fix command. 2. Add version check to run_factorial.sh as an early gate (before YAML parsing).

issue-review.title: GPU capacity constraints (cost perspective)
issue-review.assessment:
With 1 L4 GPU quota, all 22 jobs run sequentially. At ~15 min average per job, that is ~5.5 hours wall-clock (best case). With PENDING gaps, easily 8-12 hours. The controller burns ~EUR 0.40/hour during this time (n4-standard-4). Total controller cost for one debug experiment: EUR 2.20-4.80.
With 4 L4 GPU quota: 4-way parallelism, ~1.5 hours wall-clock, controller cost EUR 0.60. The quota increase request is FREE — it only costs if GPUs are actually provisioned.
Cost of PENDING: every hour of PENDING = EUR 0.13 (controller) + EUR 0 (no GPU). But the human time cost is much higher — the researcher cannot proceed with analysis until training completes.
issue-review.proposal: 1. Request quota increase: NVIDIA_L4_GPUS from 1 to 4 in europe-west4. This is a GCP support request, typically approved in 24-48 hours. Command: gcloud compute regions describe europe-west4 --project=minivess-mlops to check current quotas, then request via Cloud Console. 2. Implement cost tracking in run_factorial.sh: after each job completes, compute actual cost (duration x rate) and append to the job log. Sum at the end. Compare against budget cap. 3. For SAM3 on-demand jobs: L4 on-demand is $0.70/hr. With 16 SAM3 conditions at ~25 min each, that is 6.7 GPU-hours = $4.67. If 4-way parallel, the wall-clock is 1.7 hours but GPU cost is the same ($4.67). Parallelism saves human time, not GPU cost. 4. Consider time-of-day scheduling: GCP spot availability is typically higher at night (EU time). Launching experiments at 22:00 CET may reduce PENDING time.

issue-review.title: DVC data not on GCS (cost perspective)
issue-review.assessment: The 45-min delay had no direct cloud cost (data was downloaded to local machine, then pushed to GCS). However, the human time cost was significant — the session context was consumed by data pipeline debugging instead of experiment execution. Prevention is free (add a check), remediation is cheap (~5 min of GCS egress-free push), but the discovery cost is high (debugging why jobs fail to pull data).
issue-review.proposal: 1. Add GCS bucket size to the FinOps snapshot: if any bucket is 0 bytes, flag it as "EMPTY — data not pushed." 2. Add a cost estimate to the preflight output: "Data on GCS: 984 MB MiniVess + 1.9 GB DeepVess. Estimated storage cost: $0.06/month."

issue-review.title: Docker freshness false positives (cost perspective)
issue-review.assessment: Each false positive Docker rebuild costs: ~20 min build time (local CPU) + ~5 min push to GAR (6 GiB upload) + ~2 min GAR storage delta. No direct cloud cost (build is local), but the human time cost is ~25 min per false positive. With frequent doc/test commits (5-10 per session), this could trigger 5-10 unnecessary rebuilds per week.

issue-review.title: SAM3 weight download cost (cost perspective)
issue-review.assessment:
Each SAM3 job currently downloads 9 GB from HuggingFace during setup (~10 min). With 16 SAM3 conditions in a debug factorial, that is 16 x 9 GB = 144 GB of external network transfer per experiment run. If jobs are preempted and retry, the transfer multiplies further.
Cost comparison (per experiment, 16 SAM3 jobs): - Current (HuggingFace): $0 egress but 16 x 10 min = 160 min of setup time. With 50% preemption rate: 240 min setup. GPU idle during setup = wasted $. - Cached (GCS same-region): $0 egress (same-region free), 16 x 1-2 min = 24 min setup. Storage cost: $0.05/month (9 GB, Standard, europe-west4). - GPU cost saved: setup reduction of ~136-216 min. At L4 on-demand $0.70/hr, that is $1.59-2.52 saved per experiment just in GPU idle time during setup. - ROI: $0.05/month storage pays for itself after 1 experiment.

issue.title: GPU Capacity Constraints — europe_strict + 1 L4 Quota
issue.impact: Experiments stall indefinitely when europe-west4 L4 GPUs are unavailable. 22 sequential jobs with PENDING gaps push wall-clock from 5.5h to 8-12h. No automatic fallback, no alerting, no user notification.
issue.root-cause: Triple bottleneck: (1) europe_strict = 1 region, (2) GCP quota = 1 L4 GPU, (3) europe-west4 L4 spot pool is small and contested. Any one factor alone is manageable; all three together create a hard stall.
issue.expert-consensus: All 5 experts agree this is P0. The fix requires both quota increase (capacity) and region fallback (availability). Neither alone is sufficient.

issue.title: SkyPilot Controller Auto-Stop Too Aggressive
issue.status: OPEN — causes preflight failures and launch delays
issue.impact: Controller stops after 10 min idle. Before each experiment, user must manually restart (+2-3 min). If forgotten, preflight fails with unclear error. During inter-phase gaps (Phase 1 complete, preparing Phase 2), controller may stop and lose job state visibility.
issue.root-cause: SkyPilot default AUTOSTOP=10m. Never adjusted for this project's workflow where experiment preparation takes 15-30 min between controller use.
issue.expert-consensus: E1 and E4 agree: increase to 60 min, add auto-start in run_factorial.sh. E5 notes controller idle cost is ~EUR 0.13/hour — 60 min auto-stop adds EUR 0.13 per session vs 10 min. Negligible.

issue.title: DVC Data Not on GCS After Region Migration
issue.discovered: 2026-03-28 07:50 UTC (empty GCS buckets after europe-west4 migration)
issue.impact: 45-min delay: discover empty buckets, download MiniVess from EBRAINS, discover DeepVess also missing, download from Cornell eCommons, push both. Cloud VMs would fail at DVC pull if launched without data.
issue.root-cause: Region migration plan omitted "re-push data to new buckets" step. Pulumi creates infrastructure (buckets, registries) but does not migrate data. No post-migration verification script existed.
issue.expert-consensus: E3 and E5 agree: create post-migration verification script. E4 agrees: existing preflight already checks DVC data — ensure it has clear remediation. This is a process gap, not a code gap.

issue.title: Docker Image Freshness Check False Positives
issue.impact: Every doc/test/metalearning commit triggers a false freshness failure. Each unnecessary rebuild costs ~25 min (build + push). With frequent commits (5-10 per session), this blocks experiment launches repeatedly.
issue.root-cause: check_docker_image_freshness() compares git HEAD with image OCI label. HEAD includes ALL file changes; Docker image only contains src/, deployment/, configs/, pyproject.toml, uv.lock. Doc-only commits create false mismatch.
issue.expert-consensus: E2 proposes content-aware comparison (diff Docker-relevant paths only). E5 confirms cost justification. All agree this is a straightforward fix.

issue.title: No Automated DeepVess Downloader in Acquisition Flow
issue.status: PARTIALLY RESOLVED — downloader exists, flow integration missing
issue.discovered: 2026-03-28 08:20 UTC (DeepVess needed on GCS, no automation)
issue.impact: download_deepvess() now exists and works. But the acquisition Prefect flow does not dispatch to it automatically. User must call the function manually or use the raw script. This violates TOP-2 (zero manual work).
issue.root-cause: Acquisition flow's downloader dispatch was MiniVess-only. DeepVess was added as a test dataset with download_method="manual" placeholder. Nobody wired the automated path.
issue.expert-consensus: E3 proposes full acquisition flow integration for all 3 datasets. E4 proposes smoke test for downloader registry completeness.

issue.title: SkyPilot API Version Mismatch
issue.impact: Potential for subtle API incompatibilities between dev builds. Server did not recognize client requests correctly until restarted.
issue.root-cause: "uv sync" updates the SkyPilot package but does not restart the running API server process. The server continues running the old version.
issue.expert-consensus: E2 and E4 agree: add version check to preflight. E4 wants it as HARD FAIL. E2 suggests coupling it with venv freshness check.

issue.title: Stale .venv Paths After Repo Move
issue.impact: All venv-based scripts fail after repo directory move. SkyPilot API, preflight, and run_factorial.sh all break. Requires manual "rm -rf .venv && uv sync".
issue.root-cause: Python venvs store absolute paths in shebangs and pyvenv.cfg. Moving the parent directory invalidates all paths. No automated detection existed.
issue.expert-consensus: E2 proposes pyvenv.cfg path validation in preflight. All agree this is a one-time setup issue that should be caught early.

issue.title: SAM3 Pretrained Weights Not Cached on GCS — Setup Causes Spot Preemption
issue.status: OPEN — directly causes SAM3 spot preemption cycling
issue.discovered: 2026-03-28 (monitoring revealed SAM3 zero-shot jobs cycling during setup)
issue.impact: SAM3 zero-shot jobs get spot-preempted during setup because the setup phase takes ~15-20 min total: Docker pull from GAR (~5 min, 6 GB), SAM3 weights from HuggingFace (~10 min, 9 GB over public internet), DVC data from GCS (~3 min). The HuggingFace download is the single longest substep and uses external network (slow, variable, no GCP peering). Every preempted job restarts the full setup, creating a compounding failure loop. With 16 SAM3 conditions, this wastes 160+ minutes of setup time per experiment — more if preemptions cascade.
issue.root-cause: train_factorial.yaml setup calls hf_hub_download('facebook/sam3', 'sam3.pt') on every job. No caching layer. The 9 GB download uses external network from GCP to HuggingFace CDN. gs://minivess-mlops-checkpoints already exists in europe-west4 but is only used for trained checkpoints, not pretrained inputs.
issue.expert-consensus: All 5 experts agree this is P0. E1 (Platform): reduces setup from 15-20 min to 8-10 min, making spot viable for SAM3. E3 (Data): pretrained weights are INPUTS not artifacts — no mlflow_only_artifact_contract violation. E4 (Reliability): eliminates the primary preemption vulnerability window. E5 (Cost): $0.05/month storage saves $1.59-2.52/experiment in GPU idle time. ROI: 1 experiment.

issue.title: No Per-Model Spot/On-Demand Override
issue.impact: No further impact. run_factorial.sh reads model_overrides.use_spot from factorial YAML and passes --no-use-spot for SAM3 models.
issue.root-cause: Original run_factorial.sh used global spot=true for all models. SAM3's 80% preemption rate on 25+ min jobs made spot economically irrational.
issue.expert-consensus: Fully resolved. Document the mechanism in this plan for reference.

phase.name: P0 Blockers — GPU Capacity + Data Migration Prevention + SAM3 Weight Caching
phase.description: Fix the three issues that can stall experiments entirely: GPU provisioning bottleneck, missing data after infrastructure migration, and SAM3 spot preemption caused by 10-min HuggingFace downloads during setup.

task.title: Request GCP L4 GPU Quota Increase
task.description: Request NVIDIA_L4_GPUS quota increase from 1 to 4 in europe-west4. This enables 4-way parallel job execution, reducing wall-clock from 5.5h to ~1.5h for the debug factorial.

steps.step: Check current quota: gcloud compute regions describe europe-west4 --project=minivess-mlops --format="yaml(quotas)" | grep -A2 NVIDIA_L4

Request increase via Cloud Console: Console > IAM and Admin > Quotas > Filter: NVIDIA_L4_GPUS > europe-west4 > Request increase to 4. Justification: "Academic research platform (Nature Protocols paper). Running factorial experiments with 4 model families. 4 GPUs enables parallel training, reducing experiment time from 6h to 1.5h."
Also request PREEMPTIBLE_NVIDIA_L4_GPUS quota increase to 4 (spot instances).

task.success-criteria: Quota approval email received from GCP (24-48h typical).
task.cost-impact: Zero — quota increase is free. Only actual GPU usage is billed.

task.title: Create europe_relaxed Region Config
task.description: Create a new region config that includes 3 EU regions as fallbacks. This provides resilience against single-region capacity exhaustion while keeping data egress within EU (minimal cost: ~$0.01/GiB intra-EU).

implementation.code-spec:
# EU multi-region — europe-west4 preferred, intra-EU fallbacks. # Same-region to GCS: $0.00 egress. Cross-EU: ~$0.01/GiB (~$0.03/job). # Best balance of cost predictability and availability for debug runs. # # Compared to europe_strict: adds 2 fallback regions for capacity resilience. # Compared to europe_us: no transatlantic egress (~$0.12/GiB savings).
description: "EU multi-region — europe-west4 preferred, intra-EU fallbacks"
regions: gcp: L4: - region: europe-west4 # Netherlands — 3 zones, co-located with GAR/GCS zones: 3 - region: europe-west1 # Belgium — 2 zones zones: 2 - region: europe-west3 # Frankfurt — 2 zones zones: 2
implementation.correction: configs/cloud/regions/europe.yaml ALREADY provides the 3-EU-region fallback. No new file needed. Instead, update debug.yaml: infrastructure.region_config: europe (was: europe_strict) For cost-critical production runs, keep europe_strict in paper_full.yaml.

task.success-criteria: debug.yaml uses region_config: europe. Dry run shows 3 EU regions in ordered: block.

task.title: Add Provisioning Stall Detector to Preflight
task.description: Add a preflight check that detects the GPU capacity bottleneck BEFORE launch: (1) check GPU quota, (2) check configured regions, (3) warn if single-region + low quota.

implementation.function: 1. Read the factorial YAML's infrastructure.region_config. 2. Load the region config YAML to count configured regions. 3. Query GPU quota: "gcloud compute regions describe {region} --project=minivess-mlops --format=json" → parse quotas. 4. If configured_regions == 1 AND gpu_quota == 1: return WARNING: "Single region + 1 GPU quota = sequential execution. Consider: (a) region_config: europe for fallback, (b) request quota increase." 5. If gpu_quota == 0: return FAIL: "No L4 GPU quota in {region}." 6. Report available quota: "L4 GPU quota: {used}/{limit} in {region}."

task.success-criteria: Preflight shows GPU quota status. Warns on single-region + quota=1. Test covers all 3 paths (OK, WARNING, FAIL).

task.title: Reduce max_wait_hours for Debug Runs
task.description: Reduce max_wait_hours from 168 (1 week) to 24 (1 day) in debug.yaml. A debug experiment should not silently wait a week for GPU capacity. paper_full.yaml keeps 168h for production runs.
task.implementation: In debug.yaml, change infrastructure.max_wait_hours from 168 to 24. Add comment: "24h max wait for debug — escalate if PENDING longer."

task.title: Create Post-Migration Verification Script
task.description: Create scripts/post_migration_verify.py that validates all data and infrastructure state after a region migration. This prevents the 45-min data gap from recurring in future migrations.

checks.check: For each bucket in Pulumi outputs, verify size > 0 bytes. For DVC data bucket, verify MiniVess and DeepVess directories exist. Remediation: "Run: dvc push -r gcs"
Verify base:latest and mlflow:* images exist in the new GAR. Remediation: "Run: make build-base-gpu && docker push {gar_url}"
Verify mlflow and optuna databases exist in Cloud SQL. Remediation: "Run: gcloud sql databases list --instance={instance}"
Verify MLflow Cloud Run service returns HTTP 200. Remediation: "Check deployment: gcloud run services describe minivess-mlflow"
Verify all resources are in the same region (GAR, GCS, Cloud SQL, Cloud Run). Flag any cross-region resources.

task.success-criteria: Script runs post-migration and catches all 5 categories of gaps. Would have caught the empty GCS buckets in the 11th pass.

task.title: Cache SAM3 Pretrained Weights on GCS
task.description:
Cache SAM3 pretrained weights (sam3.pt, ~9 GB) on GCS in europe-west4 to eliminate the 10-min HuggingFace download during SkyPilot job setup. This directly addresses the spot preemption cycling observed during 11th pass monitoring: SAM3 zero-shot jobs are preempted during setup because the setup phase takes 15-20 min, with HuggingFace download being the single longest substep.
Pre-trained weights are INPUTS (like training data), NOT trained artifacts. This does NOT violate the mlflow_only_artifact_contract — that contract governs trained model outputs, not pretrained foundation model weights.

step.title: One-time upload of SAM3 weights to GCS

implementation.step:
Download sam3.pt locally (if not already cached in ~/.cache/huggingface/): python -c "from huggingface_hub import hf_hub_download; hf_hub_download('facebook/sam3', 'sam3.pt')"
Upload to GCS: gsutil cp ~/.cache/huggingface/hub/models--facebook--sam3/.../sam3.pt \ gs://minivess-mlops-checkpoints/pretrained/sam3.pt
Verify: gsutil ls -l gs://minivess-mlops-checkpoints/pretrained/sam3.pt

step.title: Update SkyPilot YAML setup to use GCS-cached weights

implementation.step:
In train_factorial.yaml, replace the HuggingFace download step with:
# Pre-trained weights: GCS cache (same-region, free, fast) with HuggingFace fallback mkdir -p /app/pretrained if gsutil cp gs://minivess-mlops-checkpoints/pretrained/sam3.pt /app/pretrained/sam3.pt 2>/dev/null; then echo "SAM3 weights loaded from GCS cache (same-region, ~1-2 min)" else echo "GCS cache miss — falling back to HuggingFace (~10 min)" python -c "from huggingface_hub import hf_hub_download; hf_hub_download('facebook/sam3', 'sam3.pt', local_dir='/app/pretrained')" fi
NOTE: The exact setup block structure depends on how train_factorial.yaml currently handles the SAM3 weight download. Read the file before modifying. Per CLAUDE.md Rule 31, this is a declarative config change — verify with the user before modifying if the exact syntax is unclear.

step.title: Update model adapter to check /app/pretrained/ first

implementation.step:
The SAM3 model adapter (src/minivess/adapters/sam3_adapter.py) should check for weights at the GCS-cached path (/app/pretrained/sam3.pt) before calling hf_hub_download. This is a Python code change, not a YAML change.
Pseudo-implementation: pretrained_path = Path("/app/pretrained/sam3.pt") if pretrained_path.exists(): weights = torch.load(pretrained_path) else: weights_path = hf_hub_download('facebook/sam3', 'sam3.pt') weights = torch.load(weights_path)

step.title: Add pretrained weight check to preflight

implementation.step: In scripts/preflight_gcp.py, add check_pretrained_weights_on_gcs(): 1. Run: gsutil ls gs://minivess-mlops-checkpoints/pretrained/ 2. Verify sam3.pt exists and size is > 1 GB (sanity check). 3. If missing: return WARNING "SAM3 pretrained weights not cached on GCS. Setup will download from HuggingFace (~10 min per job). Fix: gsutil cp gs://minivess-mlops-checkpoints/pretrained/sam3.pt"

step.title: Add pretrained weights to post-migration verify

implementation.step: In scripts/post_migration_verify.py, add a check for pretrained weights under the gcs_data_presence section: Verify gs://minivess-mlops-checkpoints/pretrained/sam3.pt exists. Remediation: "Re-upload pretrained weights after region migration."

task.success-criteria: 1. gsutil ls gs://minivess-mlops-checkpoints/pretrained/sam3.pt returns the file. 2. SkyPilot dry-run shows gsutil cp from GCS in setup block. 3. Preflight check reports pretrained weight cache status. 4. Measured setup time reduction: from ~15-20 min to ~8-10 min (Docker + GCS only). 5. SAM3 spot preemption during setup is significantly reduced.
task.cost-impact: Storage: $0.05/month (9 GB, GCS Standard, europe-west4). Egress: $0.00 (same-region transfer is free). Setup time saved: ~8-10 min per SAM3 job. With 16 SAM3 conditions: ~136 min/experiment. GPU idle cost saved: ~$1.59-2.52 per experiment (L4 on-demand rate during setup). ROI: pays for itself after 1 experiment run.

phase.name: P1 Improvements — Docker Freshness, Controller, Acquisition Flow
phase.description: Fix issues that cause significant time waste but are not experiment-blocking.

task.title: Fix Docker Image Freshness Check (Content-Aware)
task.description: Replace the naive HEAD comparison with a content-aware check that only fails when Docker-relevant files (src/, deployment/docker/, configs/, pyproject.toml, uv.lock) have changed since the image was built.

implementation.function:
Replace current implementation with:
DOCKER_RELEVANT_PATHS = [ "src/", "deployment/docker/", "deployment/skypilot/", "configs/", "pyproject.toml", "uv.lock", ]
1. Get image_commit from OCI label (existing code, unchanged). 2. Get head_commit from git rev-parse HEAD (existing code, unchanged). 3. If image_commit == head_commit: return PASS (exact match, fast path). 4. If image_commit != head_commit: a. Run: git diff --name-only {image_commit}..{head_commit} -- {paths} where {paths} = DOCKER_RELEVANT_PATHS joined by space. b. If diff output is empty: return PASS with message "Image commit {image_commit[:12]} differs from HEAD {head_commit[:12]} but no Docker-relevant files changed — image is functionally current." c. If diff output is non-empty: return FAIL with message "Docker image STALE: {N} Docker-relevant files changed since image build. Changed paths: {file_list}. Rebuild: make build-base-gpu ..." 5. Edge case: if image_commit is not an ancestor of HEAD (e.g., force push), fall back to FAIL with "Cannot determine diff — rebuild recommended."

task.success-criteria: Doc-only commits no longer trigger false freshness failures. src/ changes still correctly trigger rebuild requirement. 5 tests pass.

task.title: Auto-Start Controller in run_factorial.sh
task.description: Add controller auto-start to run_factorial.sh so the user never has to manually start the controller before launching experiments.
task.implementation:
Add after the preflight section in run_factorial.sh:
# ── Ensure SkyPilot controller is running ────────────────────────── if [ "${DRY_RUN}" = false ]; then echo "Checking SkyPilot jobs controller status..." CONTROLLER_STATUS=$("${SKY_BIN}" jobs controller status 2>&1 || echo "STOPPED") if echo "${CONTROLLER_STATUS}" | grep -qi "stopped\|not found\|error"; then echo "Controller is stopped. Starting..." "${SKY_BIN}" jobs controller start || { echo "FATAL: Failed to start SkyPilot jobs controller." exit 1 } echo "Controller started. Waiting 30s for readiness..." sleep 30 else echo "Controller is running." fi fi
Also: increase auto-stop timeout. In SkyPilot config (~/.sky/config.yaml or equivalent), set: jobs: controller: idle_minutes_to_autostop: 60
task.success-criteria: run_factorial.sh automatically starts controller if stopped. No manual "sky jobs controller start" needed before experiments. Auto-stop is 60 min (verified via sky jobs controller status).

task.title: Wire DeepVess Downloader into Acquisition Flow
task.description: Complete the acquisition flow integration so that all registered datasets (MiniVess, DeepVess, VesselNN) can be downloaded via the Prefect flow, not just via manual function calls.
task.implementation:
1. In acquisition_flow.py, update the download_datasets task to: - Read dataset names from the flow config (not hardcoded). - For each dataset, look up the downloader in _DOWNLOADERS dict. - Call the downloader function. - After download, run format conversion if needed. - After conversion, run "dvc add" + "dvc push -r gcs" if GCS is configured.
2. Add VesselNN downloader to src/minivess/data/downloaders.py: download_vesselnn() — download from the VesselNN source. Register in _DOWNLOADERS dict.
3. Add acquisition flow smoke test: - For each dataset in acquisition_registry, verify _DOWNLOADERS[name] exists. - Mock HTTP calls to verify the dispatch works without actual downloads.
4. Update acquisition_registry.py: verify all datasets have download_method != "manual".
task.success-criteria: "prefect deployment run 'acquisition-flow/default' --params '{dataset: deepvess}'" downloads DeepVess, converts format, and pushes to GCS. Smoke test verifies all 3 datasets have working dispatchers.

phase.name: P2 Hardening — Venv, API Version, Monitoring
phase.description: Add prevention mechanisms for one-time setup issues and improve monitoring.

task.title: Add Venv Path Validation to Preflight
task.description: Detect stale .venv paths early by validating pyvenv.cfg against the current repo location.

implementation.function: 1. Read .venv/pyvenv.cfg (if exists). 2. Parse "home = /path/to/python/bin" line. 3. Verify the path exists on the filesystem. 4. Also check that REPO_ROOT/.venv/bin/python exists and is executable. 5. If either check fails: return FAIL: "Stale .venv detected (repo was moved?). Fix: rm -rf .venv && uv sync --all-extras" 6. If .venv does not exist: return FAIL: "No .venv found. Run: uv sync --all-extras"

task.success-criteria: Preflight catches stale venv after repo move. Clear remediation message.

task.title: Add SkyPilot API Version Check to Preflight
task.description: Detect SkyPilot client/server version mismatch before launching experiments.

implementation.function: 1. Run "sky api version" to get server version (or detect server is stopped). 2. Run "sky --version" to get client version. 3. If server is stopped: return WARNING "SkyPilot API not running. Start with: sky api start" (not FAIL — run_factorial.sh auto-starts). 4. If server != client: return FAIL "SkyPilot API version mismatch: server={server_ver}, client={client_ver}. Fix: sky api stop && sky api start" 5. If match: return PASS "SkyPilot API: server={ver}, client={ver} (match)"

task.success-criteria: Preflight detects 12-day version mismatch. Hard fail prevents launch with mismatched API versions.

task.title: Add Provisioning Stall Notification to Experiment Report
task.description: When jobs cycle PENDING/STARTING for >15 min, write a status file that can be polled externally. This provides passive notification without requiring active monitoring.
task.success-criteria: Status file appears within 15 min of PENDING stall. File contains actionable information for the researcher.

dependency-graph.note: Tasks within each phase can be executed in parallel. Phases must be sequential (Phase 1 before Phase 2 before Phase 3).

task.note: GCS upload + YAML setup update + preflight check

cost-benefit-analysis.summary: Total estimated effort: 3-5 working days (12-17 hours of implementation). Total estimated cloud cost of fixes: $0.05/month (GCS storage for SAM3 weights). Expected time savings per experiment session: 120-210 minutes (60-90 min from parallelism/fewer rebuilds + 60-120 min from SAM3 weight caching). Expected cost savings per experiment: $1.59-7.52 (parallelism + fewer false rebuilds + GPU idle reduction from faster SAM3 setup).

issue.cost-of-not-fixing: 2-6 hours of PENDING per experiment. EUR 0.26-0.78 controller idle cost. High human time cost: researcher blocked waiting for GPUs.
issue.cost-of-fixing: 2.25h implementation. Quota increase: 0 EUR. Region config: YAML edit. Preflight check: 1h coding + testing.
issue.roi: Pays for itself in 1 experiment session.

issue.cost-of-not-fixing: 2-3 min manual restart per session. Risk of forgotten restart.
issue.roi: Pays for itself in 20 sessions (time) or immediately (risk reduction).

issue.cost-of-not-fixing: 45 min delay per migration. Risk of launching without data.
issue.roi: Pays for itself in 3 migrations. Also serves as documentation.

issue.cost-of-not-fixing: 25 min per false positive rebuild. 125-250 min/session wasted.
Manual download + push each time. ~15 min per occurrence.
issue.roi: Pays for itself in 8 uses. Also required for platform completeness.

issue.cost-of-not-fixing: Subtle API bugs. 5-15 min diagnosis per occurrence.
15-30 min diagnosis + venv recreation per occurrence.

10 min HuggingFace download per SAM3 job. 16 jobs/experiment = 160 min setup. With 50% preemption rate during setup: 240+ min wasted. GPU idle during setup costs $0.70/hr (L4 on-demand). $1.59-2.52 GPU idle cost per experiment. The long setup window is the PRIMARY cause of SAM3 spot preemption cycling.
issue.cost-of-fixing: 1h implementation. $0.05/month GCS storage (9 GB). One-time upload: 10 min (local → GCS).
issue.roi: Pays for itself after 1 experiment run. Also makes spot viable for SAM3.

testing-strategy.principle: Every new preflight check gets unit tests. Every config change gets validated by dry-run. Follow TDD (RED-GREEN-VERIFY) per CLAUDE.md Rule 2.

task.test: Dry run with europe region config shows 3 regions in ordered: block.
test_post_migration_all_ok — passes when all checks green.
test_pretrained_weights_on_gcs — PASS when sam3.pt exists on GCS.
test_pretrained_weights_missing — WARNING when sam3.pt not on GCS.
test_setup_uses_gcs_cache — verify SkyPilot YAML setup uses gsutil cp from GCS.
test_post_migration_includes_pretrained — verify post-migration script checks pretrained weights.
test_docker_freshness_doc_only — PASS when only docs/ changed.
Dry run verifies controller check logic does not block on dry-run.
Integration: run_factorial.sh --dry-run succeeds with controller stopped.
test_venv_path_valid — PASS when .venv paths are correct.
test_venv_path_stale — FAIL when pyvenv.cfg points to nonexistent path.
test_venv_missing — FAIL when .venv does not exist.
test_skypilot_server_stopped — WARNING when server not running.

testing-strategy.verification: After all tasks complete: 1. make test-staging — must pass with 0 failures, 0 skips. 2. run_factorial.sh --dry-run configs/factorial/debug.yaml — must show europe multi-region, controller check, content-aware freshness. 3. uv run python scripts/preflight_gcp.py — must show all new checks. 4. uv run python scripts/post_migration_verify.py — must pass all checks.

decision.title: Default debug region: europe (not europe_strict)
decision.rationale: europe_strict (1 region) causes PENDING stalls. europe (3 EU regions) provides fallback at minimal cost (~$0.03/job intra-EU egress). europe_strict remains available for cost-sensitive runs.

decision.title: Docker freshness: content-aware, not HEAD-based
decision.rationale: HEAD includes all commits. Docker image only includes src/, deployment/, configs/, pyproject.toml, uv.lock. Content-aware diff eliminates false positives from doc/test/metalearning changes.

decision.title: Controller auto-start in run_factorial.sh
decision.rationale: The controller is a prerequisite for SkyPilot job submission. Rather than requiring manual restart, run_factorial.sh should ensure the controller is running. The EUR 0.13/hour idle cost is negligible vs. human time.

decision.title: max_wait_hours: 24h for debug, 168h for production
decision.rationale: Debug experiments are time-sensitive (researcher waiting). 1-week wait is inappropriate — the researcher should be notified within 24h to adjust region config or try later. Production runs may legitimately wait for spot capacity.

decision.title: SkyPilot API version mismatch is a HARD FAIL
decision.rationale: Dev builds (v1.0.0.dev*) have unstable APIs. Running mismatched versions risks silent data corruption or job submission failures. The fix is trivial (sky api stop && sky api start) — hard fail is appropriate.

decision.title: Request L4 GPU quota increase to 4
decision.rationale: Current quota of 1 L4 GPU in europe-west4 forces sequential execution. With 22 jobs, wall-clock is 5.5h+ (best case). 4 GPUs enable 4-way parallelism, reducing wall-clock to ~1.5h. Quota increase is free — only actual GPU usage is billed.

decision.title: Cache pretrained model weights on GCS (same-region)
decision.rationale: SAM3 weights (9 GB) downloaded from HuggingFace every job = 10 min/job setup. This is the primary cause of SAM3 spot preemption during setup (15-20 min total). GCS same-region transfer is free and ~5x faster. Pretrained weights are INPUTS (like training data), NOT trained artifacts — this does NOT violate the mlflow_only_artifact_contract. gs://minivess-mlops-checkpoints/pretrained/ is the canonical location. Storage: $0.05/month. Approach generalizes to VesselFM and any future pretrained foundation model weights.
decision.impact: deployment/skypilot/train_factorial.yaml — setup block change. gs://minivess-mlops-checkpoints/pretrained/ — new prefix. scripts/preflight_gcp.py — new check_pretrained_weights_on_gcs().

insight.title: Region migration must include data migration
insight.pattern: Infrastructure migration (Pulumi up) creates empty containers. Data must be explicitly re-pushed to new storage locations. The migration checklist must include: (1) infra up, (2) verify infra, (3) push data, (4) verify data.

insight.title: Single-region + low-quota = hard stall
insight.pattern: europe_strict (1 region) + 1 L4 GPU quota = zero parallelism + zero fallback. Any capacity constraint stalls the entire experiment. Always configure at least 2 fallback regions for non-critical runs.
insight.prevention: Preflight GPU quota + region count check

insight.title: Docker freshness must be content-aware
insight.pattern: Git HEAD includes ALL changes. Docker images include a subset of files. Freshness checks must compare only the files that affect the image, not the entire git history.

insight.title: Preflight is the primary defense layer
insight.pattern: Every infrastructure issue discovered in the 11th pass (stale venv, API mismatch, empty buckets, image freshness) could have been caught by a preflight check. Invest in preflight = invest in all future sessions. 6 of 8 issues would have been caught or prevented by better preflight.
insight.prevention: Expand preflight to cover all prerequisites. Never bypass.

insight.title: Cache pretrained weights same-region — setup time is the enemy of spot
insight.pattern: Spot preemption during setup creates a compounding failure loop: long setup → preemption → full restart → long setup again. Every external network dependency (HuggingFace, Docker Hub, pip install) adds to setup time and increases preemption probability. Solution: cache ALL large inputs (pretrained weights, Docker images, data) in the same GCS region as the compute. The goal is to minimize setup time to minimize the preemption vulnerability window. Pretrained model weights are INPUTS (like data), not ARTIFACTS (like trained checkpoints) — caching them on GCS does not violate artifact contracts.
insight.prevention: gs://minivess-mlops-checkpoints/pretrained/ for all foundation model weights. GAR in europe-west4 for Docker images. GCS in europe-west4 for DVC data. All three should be same-region. Setup should only use same-region transfers.

insight.title: Auto-start > manual prerequisites
insight.pattern: Every manual step in the cold-start sequence is a potential failure point. The controller restart, data push verification, venv validation should all be automatic (or at least automated detection with clear remediation). Reduce the number of manual steps to zero where possible.
insight.prevention: run_factorial.sh auto-starts controller. Preflight catches the rest.

summary.already-resolved: 3 (H: spot override, C: data pushed, E: downloader created)
summary.needs-prevention: 6 (A: GPU capacity, B: controller, C: migration script, D: freshness, E: flow wiring, I: SAM3 weight caching)

modified-artifacts.artifact: scripts/preflight_gcp.py (5 new checks: GPU quota, venv paths, API version, freshness rewrite, pretrained weights)

summary.expected-outcome: After implementation: experiments launch without manual prerequisites, GPU capacity constraints are detected and worked around automatically, Docker rebuilds only happen when needed, infrastructure migrations include data verification, and SAM3 job setup drops from 15-20 min to 8-10 min (GCS-cached pretrained weights eliminate HuggingFace download). The preflight script becomes a comprehensive defense layer that catches all known failure modes. Spot preemption during SAM3 setup is significantly reduced, making spot instances viable for SAM3 zero-shot evaluation.
